title: cochlearia henryi
intro: Cochlearia henryi, also known as Henry's penny cress, is a rare and endangered plant species native to the mountains of China. It belongs to the Brassicaceae family, which includes various edible and medicinal plants. The plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, and has been shown to have potential health benefits in treating conditions such as arthritis, digestive issues, and skin problems. Cochlearia henryi can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as used in salves and ointments, although its use is often limited due to its rarity and endangered status.
